Code fragment: public final TableField<ProductGroupTableRecord, Long> PGR_ID = createField(DSL.name("pgr_id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (pgr_id) even though there's already a unique constraint on GROUP_NAME which could serve as a natural primary key. Consider using GROUP_NAME as the primary key since product group names are naturally unique and immutable, eliminating the need for a surrogate key.

Code fragment: public final TableField<ProductGroupTableRecord, Integer> VERSION = createField(DSL.name("version"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.defaultValue(DSL.inline("0", SQLDataType.INTEGER)), this, "");
Explanation: The VERSION column has a default value but is marked as NULLABLE (no .nullable(false) specified), which is inconsistent - a version column should never be NULL in practice since it has a default value. Add .nullable(false) to the column definition to prevent NULL values.
